data.title: Decibel, incubated in Aptos, is set to unveil a stablecoin, USDCBL, pegged to the dollar for onchain perps, generating yield from reserves.
data.description: A new digital token called USDCBL, pegged to the value of the US dollar, is set to be used as collateral for on-chain perpetual contracts. The protocol will retain cash and Treasury reserves to generate yield, providing stability and security to the system.

data.title: Restaurant Brands Intl (NYSE: QSR) beats Q4 earnings estimates by 2.13%, reports EPS of $0.96. Revenue up $170M YoY.
data.description: On Thursday, February 12, 2026, Restaurant Brands Intl (QSR) released its Q4 earnings at 06:30 AM. The company surpassed anticipated earnings, with an EPS of $0.96 compared to the estimated $0.94, reflecting a 2.13% increase. Additionally, revenue rose by $170.00 million from the previous year's corresponding period. In the previous quarter, the company also outperformed expectations. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Callaway Golf Co. sells Jack Wolfskin, 60% of Topgolf, now in net cash position of $680M. Exceeds Q4 & FY 2025 expectations, with 2026 revenue guidance of $1.98-2.05B and Adjusted EBITDA of $170-195M. Segment results show declines but better than expected. Plans to pay off $258M convertible notes. Maintaining zero net leverage position in 2026. Conference call held to discuss financial results and outlook. Provides non-GAAP financial information.
data.description: Callaway Golf Company has returned to its roots as a golf equipment-focused company, selling off non-golf assets and significantly improving its financial position. After selling Jack Wolfskin and a 60% stake in Topgolf, the company is now in a net cash position with $680 million cash and $480 million in debt. Q4 and Full Year 2025 showed strong results with higher than expected Net Revenue and Adjusted EBITDA. For 2026, the company expects revenue between $1.98B to $2.05B and Adjusted EBITDA of $170M to $195M. Fourth-quarter net sales were $367.5 million, down 1% primarily due to fewer product launches, offset by soft goods segment gains. Adjusted EBITDA was negative $25.1 million. Full-year net sales were $2,060.1 million, with a decrease in the Soft Goods segment. Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $222.4 million. Ongoing efforts include debt repayment, a share repurchase program, and plans to focus on margin expansion and free cash flow. The company plans to pay off its remaining debt and maintain a net cash position. In the first quarter of 2026, Callaway Golf expects consolidated net sales of $635 to $665 million and Adjusted EBITDA of $110 to $125 million. Their strategy includes providing non-GAAP financial information, such as constant currency basis, non-recurring expenses, and Adjusted EBITDA, to supplement their GAAP results. Management believes these metrics give a better understanding of the company's financial performance. For more details and access to further company information, you can visit Callaway Golf Company’s official investor relations website.

data.title: Carriage Services to release 2025 annual results on Feb 25 after market closes, with a conference call on Feb 26 at 8:00 a.m. CT.
data.description: On February 12, 2026, Carriage Services, Inc. (NYSE: CSV) revealed intentions to unveil its 2025 annual results on February 25, 2026, post-market closure. The company will host a conference call on February 26, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. Central Time, broadcasted live via webcast. This event marks a significant step in transparency for the company as they aim to provide stakeholders with key financial insights. Don't forget to tune in to the webcast for a comprehensive overview of Carriage Services' performance.

data.title: Airbnb and Expedia beat earnings expectations. Only one stock rises, the other sinks.
data.description: On Thursday, Airbnb and Expedia announced their financial results, surpassing analysts' predictions. However, only one of the companies witnessed a rise in its stock value following the report. Airbnb and Expedia both reported strong earnings, with Airbnb's stock climbing while Expedia's stock declined. This news was highlighted in an article on Investor's Business Daily, discussing the contrasting market reactions between the two companies.

data.title: Roku exceeded Wall Street's Q4 earnings target, causing stock to soar; details on Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Roku, a leading streaming video platform, exceeded Wall Street's expectations for earnings in the fourth quarter. The company's stock surged in response to the news. The post about Roku's strong fourth-quarter performance was featured on Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Comfort Systems USA (NYSE: FIX) to hold Q4 2025 conference call on Feb 20 at 10:00 am CT. Results out Feb 19 after market close.
data.description: In Houston, Comfort Systems USA, Inc. (NYSE: FIX), a prominent company offering diverse contracting services, such as heating, ventilation, air conditioning, plumbing, electrical work, piping, and controls, has set its quarterly conference call and webcast for Friday, February 20, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. Central Time. The purpose of this session is to review the financial outcomes for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. Specific figures and insights will be disclosed post-market closure on Thursday, February 19.

data.title: Roadzen's Q3 report shows earnings missed by -300%, EPS at $-0.12 versus $-0.03 estimate, revenue up $2.27M YoY.
data.description: Roadzen (NASDAQ: RDZN) released its Q3 earnings report on Thursday, February 12, 2026, at 04:00 PM. The report reveals that Roadzen fell short of expected earnings by -300.0%, posting an EPS of $-0.12 compared to an estimated $-0.03. Revenue saw a $2.27 million increase from the previous year. For a detailed past earnings analysis, investors can access the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Pinterest stock drops after hours due to Q4 results: $1.32B revenue, missed estimates. Earnings at 67 cents/share vs 69 cents. Monthly active users now 619M.
data.description: Pinterest Inc (PINS) released its Q4 financial report, revealing revenue of $1.32 billion, slightly below the expected $1.33 billion, per Benzinga Pro. The company's adjusted earnings per share were 67 cents, missing the estimated 69 cents. Total revenue increased by 14% year-over-year, with a 12% rise in global monthly active users to 619 million. Pinterest stock is currently testing crucial support levels, prompting speculation on the reasons for its dip.

data.title: Coinbase reports Q4 revenue miss during crypto downturn, fails to meet estimates.
data.description: Coinbase reported lower-than-expected results for the fourth quarter, facing challenges amidst a decline in the digital asset market. The Q4 revenue miss demonstrates the impact of the bear market on the crypto exchange.

data.title: Central Garden & Pet appoints Kay M. Schwichtenberg as Director, bringing 40+ years experience, effective March 1, 2026.
data.description: Central Garden & Pet Company has welcomed Kay M. Schwichtenberg to its Board of Directors, with her appointment effective from March 1, 2026. Schwichtenberg, bringing over 40 years of experience in the animal health sector, including 29 years with Central, served as the company's Executive Vice President, Animal & Public Health in her most recent role. Central Garden & Pet Company, a key player in the pet and garden markets, is listed on NASDAQ under the symbols CENT and CENTA.

data.title: Murphy USA Inc. announces quarterly dividend of $0.63 per share, $2.52 annually, payable on March 5, 2026.
data.description: The Board of Directors at Murphy USA Inc. (NYSE: MUSA) has announced a quarterly cash dividend for their Common Stock at $0.63 per share, marking $2.52 per share annually. This signifies a 29% rise from the Q1 2025 dividend, staying in line with the Q4 2025 dividend. The dividend will be paid out on March 5, 2026, to shareholders on record as of February 23, 2026. Murphy USA (NYSE: MUSA) is a prominent gas retailer.

data.title: Coinbase Q4 revenue at $1.78B, down 5%. Full year total revenue reaches $7.2B, up 9% YoY with double trading volume.
data.description: Coinbase Global reported its fourth-quarter financial results with total revenue at $1.78 billion, down 5% from the previous quarter, missing the estimated $1.85 billion. The company's adjusted earnings per share were 66 cents, surpassing the consensus of 64 cents. Transaction revenue was $982.7 million, down from the previous year's $1.56 billion. Subscription and services revenue reached $727.4 million, up from $641.1 million YoY. The full fiscal year saw total revenue of $7.2 billion, up 9% from the previous year. Despite a weak Q4, Coinbase demonstrated growth. The article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: DraftKings Inc reported Q4 revenue of $1.99 billion, beating estimates. Stock drops despite earnings beat.
data.description: DraftKings Inc (NASDAQ: DKNG) unveiled its Q4 financial results post-market close on Thursday. The report highlights that the company surpassed analyst expectations, with fourth-quarter revenue hitting $1.99 billion compared to the anticipated $1.98 billion. Adjusted earnings per share stood at 36 cents, outperforming the projected 14 cents per share. The total revenue surged by 43% year-over-year, attributed to sustained customer engagement, effective acquisition of new customers, and elevated Sportsbook net revenue. DraftKings' stock is on the rise, with significant positive trends noted. Details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: American Well (NYSE: AMWL) beat Q4 earnings estimates by 4.4%, reporting an EPS of $-1.52. Revenue decreased by $15.70 million.
data.description: American Well (NYSE: AMWL) released its Q4 earnings on Thursday, February 12, 2026 at 04:05 PM. The company exceeded estimated earnings by 4.4%, with an EPS of $-1.52 compared to the expected $-1.59. Revenue saw a decline of $15.70 million from the previous year. More details about the earnings report and the company's previous quarter performance can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: AIM ImmunoTech begins Rights Offering, correcting date to Feb 27, 2026, for Subscription Rights to expire at 5:00 p.m. EST.
data.description: This press release is being issued to rectify an error in an AIM press release dated February 11, 2026. The Subscription Rights must be exercised by 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time on February 27, 2026, which is the anticipated expiration date of the Rights Offering.

data.title: Supernus Pharmaceuticals (Nasdaq: SUPN) to reveal Q4 and 2025 earnings on Feb 24, 2026; a CNS-focused biopharmaceutical company.
data.description: A biopharmaceutical company based in Rockville, Maryland, Supernus Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Nasdaq: SUPN), specializing in treatments for CNS diseases, disclosed plans to release its financial and business outcomes for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025 on February 24, 2026, post-market closure.
